rulings.Case Title: JANEL MITCHELL VS. GWENDOLYN REHLING ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 3, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, OVERRULED in part. Second Cause of Action. Breach of the Implied Warranty of Habitability: the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead facts, not conclusions, supporting that notice of the dilapidations was given to Defendant and that Defendant had a reasonable opportunity to cure the issues. Fourth Cause of Action. Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress: the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of this cause of action. Fifth Cause of Action. Breach of Contract: the demurrer is OVERRULED. Plaintiff attached the contract to her complaint and therefore was not required to incorporate the material terms of the contract into the SAC as Defendant contends. (Heritage Pacific Financial, LLC v. Monroy (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 972, 993.) Additionally, and contrary to Defendant's assertion, the SAC alleges that Plaintiff performed under the contract. (SAC, 39, 40.) Sixth Cause of Action. Breach of the Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing: the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. This cause of action as pled is superfluous in light of Plaintiff's identical claim for breach of contract. (Guz v. Bechtel National Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.App.4th 317, 327.). Plaintiff shall have leave to assert a non-duplicative claim for breach of covenant to good faith and fair dealing. Eighth Cause of Action. Violation of S.F. Admin. Code Section 37.10B: the demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of this cause of action. All amendments shall be on bold font. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PALLADIAN WEST, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION FKA VS. ESPERANZA AHUMADA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 3, 2024 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT JOSHUA SLOAN DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Cross-Complaint does not suggest any amendments that would address the issues forming the basis of this Court's decision on co-cross-defendant's demurrer heard on January 5, 2024. As of the time of the filing of the late opposition, Cross-Complainant had the benefit of the Court's ruling for over 2 months, hence, had ample time to provide proposed amendment suggestions for the Court to consider in ruling on this demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. BARTHOLOMEW MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 3, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT FRANK NOLAN, ANDREW WARREN, VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. The Smith Declaration fails to demonstrate that the parties met and conferred "in person or by telephone" as required by CCP 430.41. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the Complaint is now due by May 3, 2024. The Court notes that the demurrer as filed appears to be rushed. For example, compare Points and Authorities, Notice of Demurrer and Demurrer. In the points and authorities moving party states that "the demurrer to the fourth and fifth causes of action alleged against Defendants should be sustained and Defendants FRANK NOLAN and ANDREW WARREN should be dismissed." (Moving 5:7-9). This relief as to the 4th and 5th causes of action is not mentioned in the Notice of Demurrer, Demurrer and not supported by any argument or authorities in the Points and Authorities. Additionally, had Defendants provided authorities to the Plaintiff in good faith meet and confer efforts, Plaintiff might have amended the complaint. Defendants did not do so during written exchange in advance of proper "meet and confer" and appear to not have done so in the demurrer either. See misjoinder discussion (Moving 6:19-23). If Defendant raises this issue in a subsequent response, Defendant to cite authorities on point that actually discuss employee liability and propriety of naming employees as defendants; dismissal of employees in cases where vicarious liability is alleged/proven as to the employer, etc. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
